extra.reasoning: The monotonicity property concerns toggling stiff_neck from false to true while holding all other inputs fixed, requiring acuity to never decrease. Examining every rule (r1-r7), none of them reference stiff_neck as a condition; stiff_neck is an input variable but is completely unused by the rule base. The acuity output is determined solely by the other seven inputs (slurred_speech, leg_swelling, abdominal_pain via priority-3 emergent rules; vomiting, palpitations, confusion, fever via priority-2 urgent rules) with a default of routine. Therefore, for any input assignment, flipping stiff_neck from false to true leaves all rule firing conditions unchanged, the same set of rules fires, and the resolved acuity is identical (before == after). Since equality satisfies "no lower than before" under the enum ordering (self_care < routine < urgent < emergent), the property holds for the entire input space with no counterexample.
